methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $42M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Atomic AI is a Series A biotech company that has raised $42M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies—3 public (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms in Biotech & Health at the same or adjacent stage, including Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, and Neko Health.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $84M. Peer-median benchmarking across 7 same-sector comparables produces a median of $810M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue data, which is standard for undisclosed private companies. The public sector median P/S is 6.1x across the three tickers, but without Atomic AI's revenue, this method remains inoperable.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $71M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $447M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate scaling and peer heterogeneity in the underlying comp set. The stage-progression result anchors toward the lower bound, while peer medians skew higher. Key uncertainties include Atomic AI's go-to-market traction, IP defensibility relative to the peer set, and the relevance of the observed peer valuations in a volatile biotech market.
